name: Nanobrowser
tagline: Open-source AI web automation that runs in your browser with your own API keys
metaDescription: Nanobrowser is a free, open-source Chrome extension for AI web automation. Run a multi-agent system locally with your own LLM API keys.
overview: Nanobrowser is an open-source Chrome extension that automates tasks on the web using AI. It positions itself as a free alternative to OpenAI Operator: instead of a monthly subscription, you bring your own LLM API keys and pay only for what you use. Everything runs in your local browser, so your credentials and browsing stay on your machine.

Under the hood it uses a multi-agent system. A Planner agent reasons about how to complete a task and self-corrects when it hits obstacles, while a Navigator agent carries out the actual browser steps. You can assign different models to each agent to balance cost and quality, and you interact with it through a chat side panel that shows real-time status.

It fits the Computer & Browser Use category as a browser-native option for developers and power users who want web automation without a cloud agent service. It supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Groq, Cerebras, Llama, and custom OpenAI-compatible providers, and works on Chrome and Edge.
features: Multi-agent system with a Planner and a Navigator that collaborate to complete web workflows
Bring-your-own-key model setup with support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Groq, Cerebras, Llama, and OpenAI-compatible providers
Assign different LLM models to different agents to balance performance and cost
Interactive side panel with a chat interface, real-time status updates, and conversation history
Follow-up questions about completed tasks for contextual back-and-forth
Runs locally in the browser so API keys and data never leave your machine

gettingStarted.intro: The simplest path is the Chrome Web Store, but you can also build from source if you want the latest version.

steps.heading: Install from the Chrome Web Store
steps.body: Open the Nanobrowser Chrome Web Store page, click "Add to Chrome", and confirm the installation. It works on Chrome and Edge.

steps.heading: Configure your agent models
steps.body: Click the Nanobrowser icon in your toolbar to open the side panel, open Settings (top right), add your LLM API keys, and choose which model to use for the Navigator and Planner agents.

steps.heading: Build from source (optional)
steps.body: If you prefer to build it yourself, you need Node.js v22.12.0+ and pnpm v9.15.1+. Clone the repo, install dependencies, and build the extension; the output lands in the dist directory, which you load as an unpacked extension.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/nanobrowser/nanobrowser.git
cd nanobrowser
pnpm install
pnpm build

useCases: Automating repetitive web tasks across sites, such as filling forms or gathering information, without writing a script
Running a free, local alternative to a hosted browser agent while paying only your own LLM usage
Keeping web automation private by running everything in your browser with no data sent to a third-party service
Experimenting with assigning different models to planning vs. navigation to tune cost and quality
